articles.title: ‘Equity in longevity is the entire ballgame’

The longevity sector has become rather fond of the word “personalized”; biomarker dashboards, bespoke supplementation stacks and AI-assisted diagnostics now arrive wrapped in the language of precision medicine. articles.summary: Can we finally treat joint pain without trading relief for risk? AlzeCure’s new ACD137 data hints at a shift in osteoarthritis care. What if treating chronic joint pain didn’t require choosing between effectiveness and long-term harm? That question is at the core of a new scientific publication from Swedish biotech company AlzeCure Pharma, which has […] The post New research advances non-opioid osteoarthritis pain relief appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Fitnescity pushes for higher DEXA standards
articles.summary: As body composition scans move into the longevity mainstream, Fitnescity Health wants consumers to look beyond the cheapest option. DEXA scans sat in the background of clinical medicine for years, mostly associated with bone density tests for older adults. Now, they are becoming a defining tool in the longevity conversation. Consumers using GLP-1 medications, tracking […] The post Fitnescity pushes for higher DEXA standards appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Alzheimer’s gene linked to neuronal DNA resilience
articles.summary: Buck Institute researchers link APOE2 longevity protection to DNA repair and neuronal senescence resistance. The APOE gene has occupied a peculiar position in aging research for decades; widely discussed, clinically important and yet still frustratingly incomplete in mechanistic terms. Scientists have long known that carriers of the APOE2 variant appear more likely to reach advanced […] The post Alzheimer’s gene linked to neuronal DNA resilience appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.summary: An FDA backing signals growing momentum for RNA-based therapies in oncology and longevity science. What if treatment for chronic diseases could work less like chemotherapy and more like spellcheck? That is, in some ways, the promise behind RZ-001, an experimental liver cancer therapy from South Korean biotech Rznomics that has just received a major boost […] The post FDA fast-tracks RNA editing liver therapy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Resolution Therapeutics shares positive preclinical data for liver disease cell therapy
articles.summary: Resolution Therapeutics will present new preclinical data on its engineered regenerative macrophage therapy, RTX001, in an oral presentation at the ASGCT Annual Meeting in Boston. According to the company, the data demonstrate RTX001’s pharmacology and pharmacokinetic profile, safety and tolerability in mouse models, and efficacy at reducing liver enzymes, inflammation, and fibrosis. The company claims […] The post Resolution Therapeutics shares positive preclinical data for liver disease cell therapy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: NeuroVision acquires Durin Life Sciences to expand diagnostics
articles.summary: NeuroVision announced the acquisition of Durin Life Sciences, combining Durin’s Duritect blood-based diagnostic tests with NeuroVision’s retinal imaging technology and its BrainHealth.net telehealth patient engagement platform. The combined enterprise will operate under the NeuroVision name and remain led by Steven Verdooner, co-founder and CEO, with Durin majority owner Ric Edelman joining the board. The companies […] The post NeuroVision acquires Durin Life Sciences to expand diagnostics appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

Dr Tillmann Taube, Senior Medical Director at HepaRegeniX, will present interim results from the pilot cohort of an ongoing Phase 1b/2a […] The post New data highlights how darizmetinib may help the liver regrow after surgery appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: HCW Biologics drug prevents rare lung disease in preclinical studies
articles.summary: HCW Biologics reported that HCW11-040, a second-generation pembrolizumab-based fusion immunotherapeutic, prevented bronchopulmonary dysplasia in a clinically relevant and highly stringent animal model during IND-enabling studies conducted with Queen’s University at Kingston, Ontario. According to the company, HCW11-040 is designed to block checkpoint receptors and engage costimulatory receptors to activate exhausted immune cells and eliminate high […] The post HCW Biologics drug prevents rare lung disease in preclinical studies appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Student-built system unlocks fully autonomous electroporation for 96- and 384-well workflows
articles.summary: Inside the California NanoSystems Institute at UCLA, where some of the most advanced and technical automated infrastructure on campus resides, two students saw an opportunity hiding in plain sight.

articles.title: Single-molecule RNA mapping may reveal how shape shifts steer health and disease
articles.summary: Researchers from A*STAR Genome Institute of Singapore (A*STAR GIS) have developed a new method to study individual RNA molecules and reveal how their structures influence gene regulation, a fundamental process that affects how cells function in health and disease. Their work was published in Nature Methods.

articles.title: Implantable bacteria can now be safely contained, clearing a major hurdle for fighting infection and cancer
articles.summary: Researchers have long known that bacteria could potentially be used to deliver therapeutic drugs inside the human body. However, safely and successfully carrying out such a feat in humans has been a challenge. But now, researchers from Harvard have made another step forward toward the goal of using microbes as medicine. Their recent study, published in Science, details a novel method for containing engineered bacteria to keep them from infecting their host while still successfully delivering potentially life-saving medications.

articles.title: Bacterial energy enzyme reveals dual-trigger sodium pump mechanism, offering antibiotic clues
articles.summary: The Na+-NQR enzyme is vital for energy production in pathogenic bacteria like the one that causes cholera, making it a highly promising target for new antibiotics. Researchers combined modified artificial intelligence techniques with extensive supercomputer simulations to visualize the hidden, dynamic movements of this enzyme during sodium transport.

articles.title: Experimentally validated AI model predicts virulence of tomato yellow leaf curl virus
articles.summary: A CBBL research team led by Professor Balachandran Manavalan from the Department of Integrative Biotechnology at Sungkyunkwan University has developed DeepTYLCV, an accurate and interpretable artificial intelligence model for predicting the virulence of tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV).

articles.title: Discovery could reshape RNA editing with DNA-guided CRISPR
articles.summary: A team of engineers at the University of Florida has developed a new form of CRISPR technology that could make diagnostics and treatments safer, more precise, and more affordable, while opening the door to entirely new ways of controlling disease.

articles.title: Guide DNA — not RNA — expands the CRISPR toolkit
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03138-9 Cas12 nucleases can use guide DNA instead of guide RNA, which switches their targets from DNA to RNA.

articles.title: DNA-guided CRISPR–Cas12 for cellular RNA targeting
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03129-w ΨDNA guides are used with Cas12 for precise, programmable targeting of cellular RNA.

articles.title: Widespread DNA off-targeting confounds RNA chromatin occupancy studies
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03130-3 Many long noncoding RNA–DNA binding peaks detected using common assays arise from technical artifacts.

articles.title: Engineered proteins store digital files with 30 times density at one-tenth cost
articles.summary: Massive volumes of digital data are generated every day from AI training, big data analytics and smart devices. As conventional hard drives and cloud storage are increasingly constrained by high costs, limited capacity, high power consumption and short lifespans, molecular data storage has emerged as a breakthrough storage alternative.

articles.title: Crypto longs lose $500 million as bitcoin slides to $78,000, SOL and XRP down 5%
articles.summary: A long-skewed liquidation cascade flushed leverage across the major tokens overnight, with the move tracking a global bond selloff and the worst session for U.S. stocks since March.

articles.title: Bhutan ‘doesn’t recall’ selling any bitcoin, disputing widely-tracked $1 billion BTC drawdown
articles.summary: Arkham Intelligence data shows that over $1 billion in bitcoin has left wallets attributed to Bhutan in the past year, flowing to exchanges and trading firms. The country says it has not sold any.

articles.title: US CLARITY Act brings ‘major spike of euphoria’ to Bitcoin: Santiment
articles.summary: Santiment warned that the crypto market “typically” moves against crowd expectations, pointing to a recent rise in bullish sentiment driven by renewed momentum around the US CLARITY Act.

articles.title: House committee leaders urge Trump to nominate CFTC members, citing CLARITY Act
articles.summary: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is currently headed by Chair Michael Selig, with no public statement from Donald Trump about fully staffing the five-member panel of commissioners.

articles.title: Bitcoin Depot filing casts doubt on company’s future amid lawsuits
articles.summary: The crypto ATM company reported financial difficulties amid a changing regulatory environment and ongoing litigation, which have cost it millions of dollars.

articles.title: ICE, CME press US regulators to 'rein in' Hyperliquid energy trading: Report
articles.summary: The Hyperliquid decentralized exchange allows anyone who stakes 500,000 HYPE tokens, valued at roughly $22.2 million, to deploy new markets.

articles.title: U.S. House lawmakers who oversee the CFTC are urging Trump to fill the commission
articles.summary: As the Commodity Futures Trading Commission takes on a growing task to police U.S. crypto trading, senior lawmakers are saying it needs bipartisan leadership.

articles.title: Solayer launches Visa-compatible card for USDC payments
articles.summary: The new card lets users spend USDC balances through online, in-store and contactless transactions while accessing ATM withdrawals in supported regions.

articles.title: Crypto market structure bill still faces significant hurdles despite Senate committee win: analysts
articles.summary: TD Cowen raised the probability of the bill passing to 40% from 33% while Benchmark said the Clarity Act will need more Democratic support.

articles.title: It’s a ‘new era’ for crypto users who ‘trade anything and everything’ as Jito goes consumer, CEO Bruder says
articles.summary: Jito founder Lucas Bruder told The Block that there's a new class of users coming onchain who "want to trade anything and everything."
